title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Barbados origin
description: Barbados 1640s — rum (kill-devil) first distilled from sugar-molasses byproduct on British sugar plantations. Built on enslaved labour.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Barbados origin
description: Jamaica — high-ester funky rum style emerges. Appleton Estate oldest running distillery 1749.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Barbados origin
description: Puerto Rico — Bacardí moves here from Cuba after 1959 revolution. Today the world's #1 rum brand.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: New England rum + slave triangle
description: Boston — 150+ rum distilleries by 1750. Rum → West Africa → enslaved people → Caribbean → molasses → Boston.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: New England rum + slave triangle
description: West African coast — enslaved Africans exchanged for rum, among other goods.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: New England rum + slave triangle
description: Caribbean sugar islands — forced labour cycle continues.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Royal Navy grog ration (1655–1970)
description: London — Royal Navy grog ration (rum + water + citrus) daily for 315 years. Ended 'Black Tot Day' 31 July 1970.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Royal Navy grog ration (1655–1970)
description: Bridgetown Barbados — main naval supply port in the Caribbean.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: rum etymology
description: English 'rum' — possibly from 'rumbullion' (17th-century slang for a tumult / riot) or from 'saccharum' (Latin for sugar).

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: rum etymology
description: Brazilian Portuguese — 'cachaça' is the Brazilian cane spirit, legally distinct from rum.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Puerto Rico — 70% of all rum sold in the US is Puerto Rican (Bacardí).

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Brazil — cachaça production is massive (~1.4B litres/year) but ~99% consumed domestically.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Jamaica — premium ester-heavy rum, export focus.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Martinique — Rhum Agricole AOC (only cane-juice-based rum with legal appellation).
